solution.rawText:
We have log10 (31−n ) = (1 − n) log10 3, so the sum becomes
                            100
                            X                       100
                                                    X
                                  log10 (31−n ) =         (1 − n) log10 3
                            n=1                     n=1
                                                               100
                                                               X
                                               = (log10 3)           (1 − n).
                                                               n=1


Now the sum is actually an arithmetic series: expanding it gives
                         100
                         X
                               (1 − n) = 0 + (−1) + (−2) + · · · + (−99)
                         n=1
                                      = −(0 + 1 + 2 + · · · + 99)
                                      = − 12 × 100(0 + 99 × 1)
                                      = −4950

using the formula Sn = 12 n(2a + (n − 1)d). (Alternatively, we could have used the formula for
the nth triangular number.)
Therefore the original sum evaluates to −4950 log10 3, which is option A.




Version 1.0, July 2023                                                                Page 13
